page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores running at a speed of 3.2 GHz. The system is configured with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, which provides a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 9 Gbps.

The processor architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and integer operations. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 65056.32 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 19030.0. Memory performance is also notable, with cached read-only small block bandwidth reaching 4111.32 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth measured at 95.0 GB/sec.

This hardware profile is designed for compute-heavy tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It demonstrates high efficiency in text processing, software compilation, and cryptography, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU encryption test score of 52712.57. Additionally, the instance supports moderate LLM inference tasks, achieving 30.87 tokens/sec for text generation on a medium 7B model, though performance drops to 3.46 tokens/sec on larger 70B models. This makes it highly suitable for batch processing, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, offering a 2.0 GB per core ratio, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 9 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong performance, including a Passmark CPU score of 65056.32 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 19030.0. The processor's support for AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions enhances its efficiency for vector and matrix calculations. This instance is well-suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, web serving, data decompression, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 9 Gbps network baseline, designed for high-throughput computational workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for high-throughput, instruction-heavy workloads.

96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix math
